inline_review:
commit 36139818bf6b2abe505ab824b951e904796bcd67
Author: Liew Rui Yan <aethernet65535@gmail.com>

mm/damon: add synchronous commit for commit_inputs

This patch modifies the commit_inputs sysfs parameter to synchronously apply
parameters using damon_call(). It adds custom module parameter callbacks to
return validation errors immediately to userspace.

Link: https://lore.kernel.org/20260318153731.97470-1-aethernet65535@gmail.com

> diff --git a/mm/damon/lru_sort.c b/mm/damon/lru_sort.c
> index 554559d72976..3af6854ba3da 100644
> --- a/mm/damon/lru_sort.c
> +++ b/mm/damon/lru_sort.c

[ ... ]

> @@ -349,18 +348,46 @@ static int damon_lru_sort_apply_parameters(void)
>  	return err;
>  }
>  
> -static int damon_lru_sort_handle_commit_inputs(void)
> +static int damon_lru_sort_commit_inputs_fn(void *arg)
>  {
> +	return damon_lru_sort_apply_parameters();
> +}
> +
> +static int damon_lru_sort_commit_inputs_store(const char *val,
> +					      const struct kernel_param *kp)
> +{
> +	bool commit_inputs_request;
>  	int err;
> +	struct damon_call_control control = {
> +		.fn = damon_lru_sort_commit_inputs_fn,
> +	};
>  
> -	if (!commit_inputs)
> +	err = kstrtobool(val, &commit_inputs_request);
> +	if (err)
> +		return err;
> +
> +	if (!commit_inputs_request)
>  		return 0;
>  
> -	err = damon_lru_sort_apply_parameters();
> -	commit_inputs = false;
> -	return err;
> +	/*
> +	 * Skip damon_call() if ctx is not initialized to avoid
> +	 * NULL pointer dereference.
> +	 */
> +	if (!ctx)
> +		return -EINVAL;

During kernel boot, start_kernel() uses parse_args() to evaluate the kernel
command line. If damon_lru_sort.commit_inputs=Y is passed, parse_args()
invokes this callback before the module init function runs, so ctx is NULL.

Since returning an error from parse_args() during boot causes it to skip
parsing remaining arguments, does this regression break the system boot
process by dropping after_dashes arguments intended for the init process?

> +
> +	err = damon_call(ctx, &control);

Since this function acts as a module parameter .set callback, it is invoked
by param_attr_store() with the global param_lock held.

Could this cause a system-wide deadlock of module operations? If a sysfs
write occurs exactly during kdamond thread termination (when kdamond_fn()
cancels pending calls but before ctx->kdamond is set to NULL under
kdamond_lock), damon_is_running() can falsely return true. This would leave
the sysfs thread waiting forever on wait_for_completion() while holding
param_lock.

Additionally, prior to this patch, user scripts could configure parameters
and write 'Y' to commit_inputs even if the monitor was disabled, and they
would be applied when started.

Because damon_call() immediately returns -EINVAL if the kdamond thread is
not currently running, will this regression break existing userspace
configuration scripts that trigger a commit before enabling the monitor?

> +
> +	return err ? err : control.return_code;
>  }
>  
> +static const struct kernel_param_ops commit_inputs_param_ops = {

The previous module_param(..., bool) macro used param_ops_bool, which
includes the KERNEL_PARAM_OPS_FL_NOARG flag. This allows parameters to be
provided as a standalone flag without an explicit value.

By omitting this flag here, does providing the parameter without an explicit
value now produce a parsing error?

> +	.set = damon_lru_sort_commit_inputs_store,
> +	.get = param_get_bool,
> +};

The commit_inputs_store function parses the input into a local variable
commit_inputs_request but never writes to the global commit_inputs variable.

Because param_get_bool reads the unmodified global commit_inputs variable,
will sysfs reads of commit_inputs unconditionally return 'N'?

[ ... ]

(Note: The same regressions appear to exist in mm/damon/reclaim.c)
output:
{"findings":[{"problem":"Unkillable D-state hung task and system-wide deadlock of module operations due to sysfs param lock.","severity":"Critical","severity_explanation":"The new `commit_inputs_store()` uses `damon_call()` to synchronously apply parameters. As a module parameter `.set` callback for a built-in module, it is invoked by `param_attr_store()` with the global `param_lock` held. First, `damon_call()` blocks on a completion waiting for the `kdamond` thread, effectively blocking all module load/unload and parameter accesses system-wide for up to `sample_interval` seconds. Second, a race condition exists during `kdamond` thread termination: `kdamond_fn()` cancels pending calls with `kdamond_call(ctx, true)`, but the `ctx->kdamond` pointer isn't set to `NULL` until later under `kdamond_lock`. If a sysfs write to `commit_inputs` occurs in this exact window, `damon_is_running()` falsely returns true, causing the sysfs thread to wait forever on `wait_for_completion()`. The exiting `kdamond` thread will never signal the completion, leaving the sysfs thread permanently deadlocked. Because it holds `param_lock`, this permanently deadlocks all module operations and `/sys/module/*/parameters/` accesses across the entire system."},{"problem":"Kernel boot parameter parsing failure due to uninitialized context.","severity":"High","severity_explanation":"The patch replaces the standard boolean module parameter with a custom `.set` callback. During kernel boot, `start_kernel()` uses `parse_args()` to evaluate the kernel command line. If `damon_reclaim.commit_inputs=Y` or `damon_lru_sort.commit_inputs=Y` is present, `parse_args()` invokes the `.set` callback. Because the module `init` functions have not yet executed, the global `ctx` pointer is `NULL`. The callback explicitly checks `if (!ctx)` and returns `-EINVAL`. Returning an error from `parse_args()` during boot causes it to return an error pointer, which prompts `start_kernel()` to skip parsing any remaining arguments intended for the `init` process (the `after_dashes` arguments). This breaks backwards compatibility with existing boot configurations and can severely impact the system boot process."},{"problem":"Writing to `commit_inputs` when the monitor is inactive fails with `-EINVAL`, breaking existing configuration scripts.","severity":"Medium","severity_explanation":"Prior to this patch, user scripts could configure DAMON parameters and write `Y` to `commit_inputs` even if the monitor was disabled (`enabled=N`); the parameters would be successfully saved and seamlessly applied when the monitor was subsequently started. The new synchronous mechanism uses `damon_call()`, which immediately returns `-EINVAL` (via `damon_call_handle_inactive_ctx`) if the `kdamond` thread is not currently running. This unexpected behavior change breaks existing userspace scripts that unconditionally trigger a commit before enabling the monitor."},{"problem":"Missing `KERNEL_PARAM_OPS_FL_NOARG` flag breaks implicit boolean syntax.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"Previously, `commit_inputs` was defined via `module_param(..., bool)`, which uses the core `param_ops_bool` structure containing the `.flags = KERNEL_PARAM_OPS_FL_NOARG` flag. This flag allows the parameter to be provided as a standalone flag without an explicit value. By switching to `module_param_cb` with a custom `kernel_param_ops` structure and omitting this flag, providing the parameter without an explicit value now produces a parsing error (`requires an argument`). This is a minor ABI regression."},{"problem":"Sysfs read of `commit_inputs` is dead code and unconditionally returns 'N'.","severity":"Low","severity_explanation":"The patch changes the `.set` callback to parse the input into a local `commit_inputs_request` variable, but it never actually writes the value back to the global `commit_inputs` variable. However, it still uses the standard `param_get_bool` for its `.get` callback, which reads the unmodified global `commit_inputs` variable (which is initialized to false). While `commit_inputs` effectively acts as a write-only trigger now, unconditionally returning `N` via sysfs reads is confusing and breaks the standard semantics of boolean module parameters."}],"fixes":"","review_inline":"commit 36139818bf6b2abe505ab824b951e904796bcd67
Author: Liew Rui Yan <aethernet65535@gmail.com>

> diff --git a/mm/damon/lru_sort.c b/mm/damon/lru_sort.c
> index 554559d72976..3af6854ba3da 100644

> +++ b/mm/damon/lru_sort.c

[ ... ]

> @@ -349,18 +348,46 @@ static int damon_lru_sort_apply_parameters(void)
>  	return err;
>  }
>  
> -static int damon_lru_sort_handle_commit_inputs(void)
> +static int damon_lru_sort_commit_inputs_fn(void *arg)

>  }
